content.problemStatement:
There is a group of NNN friends who wish to enroll in a course together. The course has a maximum capacity of MMM students that can register for it. If there are KKK other students who have already enrolled in the course, determine if it will still be possible for all the NNN friends to do so or not.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case consists of a single line containing three integers NNN, MMM and KKK - the size of the friend group, the capacity of the course and the number of students already registered for the course.


Output Format
For each test case, output `Yes` if it will be possible for all the NNN friends to register for the course. Otherwise output `No`.


You may print each character of `Yes` and `No` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `YES` will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers N, M and K — the number of friends, the maximum capacity of the course and the number of students already registered.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if all N friends can register (i.e., N + K ≤ M), otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 2 + 27 = 29 ≤ 50 → Yes.\nTest case 2: 5 + 38 = 43 > 40 → No.\nTest case 3: 100 + 0 = 100 ≤ 100 → Yes.
First case: 1 + 0 = 1 ≤ 1 → Yes. Second case: 1 + 1 = 2 > 1 → No.

hidden.label: edge case full capacity already taken

solution.approach: For each test case we just need to check whether the total number of students after the friends register (N + K) exceeds the course capacity M. If N + K ≤ M we print "Yes", otherwise "No". This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); M = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        out.append('Yes' if N + K <= M else 'No')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
